t_ch: 根据积攒的灰尘和完好的封装来看，被投诉者根本没有把它们打开过，随手丢弃在了角落里。
t_en: Based on the dust accumulated and the perfectly intact seals, the complainee did not even bother opening them and just chucked them aside into a corner.

t_ch: 似乎是那刻夏的资料？值得注意，风堇也应该来看看。
t_en: This is... about Anaxa? I should read this. Hyacine, you should come have a look, too.

t_ch: 咳咳，好多灰尘…这些都是他收到的投诉信吗？
t_en: Ack, it's so dusty in here... Whoa, are these all complaints about him?

t_ch: 不用打开也知道，里头一定是「那刻夏不敬神明，其人的存在就是对社会秩序的威胁」…诸如此类。
t_en: I can already see what they must have written: "Anaxa has no respect for the gods! His very existence undermines order in our society!" ...They're probably all things like that.

t_ch: 他的影响力这么大？
t_en: Does Anaxa hold that much influence?

t_ch: 不然怎么会被称作「大表演家」呢？据说老师创立学派后的第一件事，就是当着所有人的面宣读自己的著作——
t_en: Why else would they call him "the Great Performer"? When Anaxa established his school of thought, the first thing he apparently did was read his own work to everyone.

t_ch: 「至于泰坦，它们真的符合凡人对神的认知？神造的世界必定是完美的，可翁法罗斯却遍布愚钝和丑陋！」
t_en: "Are the Titans truly an accurate reflection of what humans think gods should be? Worlds created by gods are indubitably perfect, but what of Amphoreus? Only foolishness and insensitivity everywhere you turn!"

t_ch: …很有他的风格，然后呢？
t_en: ...An uncanny impersonation. What happened after?

t_ch: 他没能讲完，就被押上了审判台。
t_en: They marched him straight to the seat of judgment before he could finish.

t_ch: 说是审判，其实是辩论。他需要一一回应学者们的控诉，否则就要被逐出树庭。
t_en: Though what he took on in the end was more a debate than an actual trial. He had to address every one of the scholars' allegations, or face expulsion from the Grove.

t_ch: 第一项控诉是：咳咳——「你是否同意泰坦们塑造了天地，山川海洋便是它们的杰作？」
t_en: The first allegation was... ahem. "Do you agree that since the Titans created the heavens and earth, so the mountains, rivers, and seas must likewise be their creations?"

t_ch: …是要我站在那刻夏的立场作出回答吗？
t_en: ...Am I supposed to put myself in Anaxa's shoes and answer that?

t_ch: 我同意。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 依照神话所述，海洋、天空、大地都是泰坦的造物，确凿无疑。
t_en: As told in the myths, the seas, the skies, and the earth are all, beyond the shadow of a doubt, creations of the Titans.

t_ch: 如果老师也能这样回答，恐怕就不用受难了。但他却以逐火的历史为例，宣扬人类也有接过神权的可能性。
t_en: Anaxa would've probably been spared a lot of trouble if he had answered this way... Instead, he quoted past instances of the Flame-Chase Journey and posited that humans have the potential to take over the divinity of Titans.

t_ch: 我反对。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 从逐火的历史来看，人类也有接过神权的可能性。
t_en: Based on the history of the Flame-Chase Journey, humans have the potential to take over the divinity of Titans.

t_ch: 这也是老师当时的观点。
t_en: Anaxa said the same thing then.

t_ch: 反对者自然对这个回答很不满意，于是便有了第二项控诉。
t_en: Needless to say, his critics didn't take kindly to his answer, so they followed up with a second allegation.

t_ch: 「你以研究灵魂之名玷污灵魂，进行神所憎恶的交易，你是否承认？」
t_en: "You claim to be studying souls, yet you are, in fact, defiling them and committing acts condemned by the gods. Do you admit it?"

t_ch: 相当严肃的指控，他还做过这种事？
t_en: That's a serious allegation. Did he really do that?

t_ch: 老师永远戴着眼罩，连独处时都不摘下，有人说他是把一只眼睛用作了炼金材料。
t_en: Anaxa always wears an eye patch, never removing it, even when alone. Some say he used one of his eyes as an alchemical ingredient.

t_ch: 世间的人都由{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}庇护，尤其是沐浴神血的黄金裔。倘若真有此事，无疑是亵渎神明。
t_en: Every being in the world owes their safekeeping to {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, and this is even more so for the Chrysos Heirs who have divine blood coursing through them. If he has really done something of that nature, then it'd indeed be sacrilegious.

t_ch: 幸好，因为没有证据，这项控诉最终不了了之。
t_en: Thankfully, they had no way to prove it, so it amounted to nothing more than an allegation in the end.

t_ch: 老师就这么舌战群儒，与挑战者一一辩论。直到最后，一位学者问他：「阿那克萨戈拉斯，你是否不敬神灵？」
t_en: One by one, Anaxa engaged in wars of words against those who would challenge him. Toward the end, a scholar asked him, "Anaxagoras, have you any respect for the gods?"

t_ch: 他沉默了几秒，回答道：「我从不否认泰坦应是伟大之物。」
t_en: It was only after a brief silence that he answered, "I have never denied that Titans are meant to be noble constructs."

t_ch: 显然话里有话。他的潜台词是，泰坦并不伟大。
t_en: What he didn't say — that the Titans are not noble — rings louder in this case.

t_ch: 嗯…我不知道那场骚动是如何平息的，但老师最终还是留在了树庭。即便被视作异端，他也在继续自己的研究。
t_en: Yes... I'm not sure how that controversy was eventually resolved, but what's for certain at least is that Anaxa was allowed to stay in the Grove where he continued his research, despite being regarded as a heretic.

t_ch: 对了，这沓投诉信提醒了我，树庭里还有个地方，或许存在石版中都查不到的线索……
t_en: By the way, this stack of complaint letters has reminded me of a place in the Grove... We may find clues there that aren't on the teleslate.

t_ch: 根石之间，那刻夏老师的炼金实验室。
t_en: I'm talking about Anaxa's alchemy lab, hidden between the cornerstones.

t_ch: 以树庭的藏书风格，竟会有这种眼皮底下的遗漏？
t_en: Given the Grove's meticulous approach to archiving, could there really be such an oversight right under their noses?

t_ch: 这是个秘密：七贤人除去首席虚设，其余列位各自享有一处禁地，供他们在遐思之余，进行不会被干扰的研究。
t_en: It's a well-kept secret. Among the Seven Sages, excluding the nominal Chief Sage — each has a private sanctuary where they can conduct research undisturbed in their free time.

t_ch: 就连友爱之馆的管理员也不能越过禁令，收录贤人的课题。不过现在……
t_en: Even the administrators of the Library of Philia are restricted from accessing those places and documenting the sages' research. But...

t_ch: 树庭都成这样了，小小打破一下禁令也没事吧…对不住啦那刻夏老师。
t_en: With the Grove in this state, I'm sure Anaxa won't be mad even if we sneak into the lab for a short while... Right?

t_ch: 我们到了，不过…这里居然什么都没有了？
t_en: This is the place, but... where is everything?

t_ch: 炼金材料，法阵图样，甚至禁令的封印也不在了…难道都被黑潮摧毁了？
t_en: Materials for alchemical experiments, matrix diagrams, and even the seal to the lab... They're all gone. Did the black tide destroy it all?

t_ch: 难怪，这一路连点像样的防备都没有。
t_en: No wonder we didn't see any defense mechanisms along the way.

t_ch: 你之前进入过这里么？
t_en: Have you ever been here before?

t_ch: 没有，甚至从未接近过。炼金是高危活动，那刻夏老师又钟情古怪的材料。学生们经常见他好好地进去，却灰头土脸甚至满脸炸毛地出来，自然不敢靠近这里。
t_en: No, not even close. Not only are alchemy experiments high-risk activities, but Professor Anaxa also has a penchant for using bizarre materials. Students often saw him walk in looking right as rain but come out covered in soot with his hair singed. Naturally, no one dared to come near this place.

t_ch: …咦？唯独那边的书柜，保存得很完整呢。
t_en: Huh? That bookshelf over there seems to be the only thing still intact.

t_ch: 这是阿那克萨戈拉斯申请调取的资料。数份不同领域的资料被收纳于一处，其中包括「泰坦造物」、「灵魂的普遍性」、「炼金学说」…不一而足。
t_en: These are the materials requested by Anaxagoras. Documents from various fields have been compiled in one place, including "Titan Creations," "The Universality of the Soul," "Alchemy Doctrine," and so on.

t_ch: 大部分资料看起来仍然属于「智种学派」的研究范围，不过，一份名为「黄金裔背景考察」的手记，夹杂在其中。
t_en: Most of the materials look to be within the research domain of the Nousporist school of thought. However, a document titled A Study on the Chrysos Heirs' Background is mixed within.

t_ch: 这是怎么回事…奥赫玛的大家，还有我？连我也在老师的研究范围内？
t_en: What's going on...? Anaxa is conducting research on our friends in Okhema and... me?

t_ch: 这份手记，有不少值得细究的地方——
t_en: This journal contains several intriguing points...

t_ch: 泰坦造物…
t_en: Creations of Titans...

t_ch: 从命运重渊到悬锋城，我已见过许多泰坦造物，树庭里也有不少{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}创造的生灵。
t_en: From the Abyss of Fate to Castrum Kremnos, I've seen many a Titan creation during my time in Amphoreus. Lifeforms created by {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} can also be found in the Grove.

t_ch: 可是通过炼金术将它们与人的灵魂融合？我很难想象……
t_en: But fusing them with human souls through alchemical means? I can't even begin to imagine...

t_ch: 黄金裔…
t_en: The Chrysos Heirs...

t_ch: 那刻夏格外关注黄金裔流淌的「神性」，还对英雄们的背景做了细致考察。
t_en: Anaxa seems to hold special interest in the divinity coursing through the Chrysos Heirs' veins, and has even conducted a thorough investigation into the heroes' backgrounds.

t_ch: 如果只有蝶宝还不奇怪，她也对自己的出身感到好奇。智种学派炼制的药剂中也有疗愈疾病的万能药，研究长生的黄金裔合情合理。
t_en: It wouldn't have been so strange if he had focused only on Cassie, given her own curiosity about her origins. Plus, considering that the Nousporists have crafted omnipotent medicines capable of curing diseases, it also makes sense that he would conduct research on immortal Chrysos Heirs.

t_ch: 可现在看来，老师的目标绝不是制药，是在究明「金血」的由来与原理？
t_en: But now, it seems Anaxa's objective was never to create medicine but to identify the origin of the golden blood and figure out its inner workings.

t_ch: 那刻夏的研究…
t_en: Anaxa's research...

t_ch: 那刻夏想通过炼金，让黄金裔体内的「金血」更加纯粹，以至抵达神性。
t_en: Through alchemy, Anaxa aims to refine the golden blood in the Chrysos Heirs to a degree of purity equal to "divinity."

t_ch: 这就是「趋近于至纯」的含义吧。可是…将泰坦造物和人体都视为炼金的材料，未免有些太过疯狂了。
t_en: This must be what he means by "close to true purity." But... treating both Titan creations and the human body as materials to be used in alchemy experiments? That's rather extreme.

t_ch: 丹宝，我有一种不太好的预感。你看这里……
t_en: Dannie, look at this. I have a bad feeling about this...

t_ch: 最担心的事情…果然还是发生了。一路看下来，我的猜想也在慢慢成型。
t_en: My biggest fear... It's come true... After going through his notes, I think I can piece it all together now:

t_ch: 那刻夏老师，在进行「灵魂炼成」的研究。
t_en: Anaxa was carrying out experiments on soul transmutation.

t_ch: 如果我没理解错，是通过炼金术将人的灵魂重新炼成？
t_en: Do you mean he's trying to reconstruct human souls alchemically?

t_ch: 这就是老师的秘密研究，不能为其他人所知的亵渎实验。比起他在公众面前的发言，这才是真正的「渎神」……
t_en: Yes, this is what he was secretly conducting research on — something he had to keep hidden from others. Compared to his public speeches, this is the real "blasphemy"...

t_ch: 啊！难道说……
t_en: Ah! I just thought of something...

t_ch: 怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: 你还记得吗？黑潮抵近时，树庭本打算转移{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的火种，可中途遭遇意外，那刻夏老师也以身殉道……
t_en: Do you remember? When the black tide was approaching, the Grove had planned to send {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}' Coreflame to a safe place, but an accident happened along the way, and Anaxa lost his life in the process...

t_ch: 但{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}征用了他的身躯，令那刻夏「死而复生」，才造成了如今的局面。
t_en: But {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} took over his body and "resurrected" him, which led to the situation we're in now.

t_ch: 没错。可如果…那刻夏老师与「理性」火种的结合，并不是一场「意外」呢？
t_en: That's right... but what if Professor Anaxa's fusion with the Coreflame of Reason wasn't an accident?

t_ch: 什么意思？
t_en: What do you mean?

t_ch: 如果你是他，在黑潮逼近，眼看自己生命无多的情况下…一定会采取的行动是什么？
t_en: Put yourself in his shoes. Knowing that the black tide was closing in and your time was running out, what would you have done?

t_ch: …亲手实践这些猜想，不计代价。
t_en: ...I'd put my theories to the test, no matter the cost.

t_ch: 对。<color=#dbc291ff>在黑潮入侵时，老师前往启蒙王座的真正目的…是打算施行炼成，将自身与泰坦的火种熔合。</color>
t_en: Exactly. <color=#dbc291ff>When the black tide came, the true reason Anaxa headed to the Luminary Throne was... to transmute and fuse himself with the Titan's Coreflame.</color>

t_ch: 所以，他成功了？
t_en: Did he succeed?

t_ch: 不，从事后的反应来看，现状应该在老师预料之外。可能他也没想到{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}会做出相似的决定。
t_en: No. Judging from how he reacted after the event, the results came as a surprise. Maybe he failed to consider the possibility that {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} would make a similar decision.

t_ch: 炼金术式的手稿被带走了，说明他的研究仍在继续……
t_en: The manuscripts detailing all the alchemical formulae have been taken away. His research... is still ongoing.

t_ch: 会是另有所用吗？
t_en: Could he have taken them for other purposes?

t_ch: 可是，用来做什么呢？留在世间的泰坦，也只剩下……
t_en: Like what? There's only one Titan left in the world now—

t_ch: 这…不会吧……
t_en: No... no way...

t_ch: 「应掌握造物的原理，令万物诞生时即为完美的状态」…如今那刻夏身在奥赫玛，他的目标……
t_en: "To conceive an entity of perfection, one must first understand the principles of creation"... If Anaxa is in Okhema now, then that means...

t_ch: ——是{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}！
t_en: ...He's after {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 老师，我们看到的这片天空是假的，对吧？
t_en: Sir, this sky above us... It's not real, is it?

t_ch: 当然，是假的。这只是教学装置，不起眼的魔术。
t_en: Of course not. This is just an image from a projection device used for educational purposes. A simple trick.

t_ch: 如今昼夜失序，为了方便你们理解星空，天文学者借助泰坦神迹，变换光线，将「过去的夜晚」投影在这里……
t_en: The day-night cycle has been lost to this world. So that students like you may still learn about the night sky, astronomers have harnessed the Titans' miraculous power to manipulate the light, projecting the "nights of the past" here for you.

t_ch: 正如圣城的天空，也是由{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的黎明机器点亮的。
t_en: Much like how the sky above the holy city is also illuminated by {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}'s Dawn Device.

t_ch: 所以，奥赫玛的天空也是虚假的。
t_en: So, the skies of Okhema are fake, too.

t_ch: 不能这么定性，那刻夏。你要知道：只有孩子才会揭开魔术的幕布，并以此为傲。
t_en: You mustn't conclude that, Anaxa. You should know that only a child would expose a trick and be proud of it.

t_ch: 很多时候，人们并非真正无知，而是必须装作无知。
t_en: Often, people know more than they let on. They simply choose to feign ignorance because they have no other choice.

t_ch: 这是自欺欺人。民众信奉{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的创世神话，祭司们说什么，他们就信什么。可是，你和我，我们已从蒙昧的洞穴里醒转。
t_en: Then they're just deluding themselves. People believe in {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}'s tale of genesis, accepting wholesale whatever the priests preach, but we're different. We've already walked out of the fire-lit cave of ignorance.

t_ch: 我们已经意识到，自己身处在一个由泰坦编织规则的世界，满眼尽是它们投下的「星空」。与其守望这片虚假的永夜，我为何不能成为操作投影仪器的那个人？
t_en: We have already realized that we live in a world where Titans make the rules. Even when we look at the sky, we're only seeing what they allow us to see. Rather than gazing at this mockery of a night sky fabricated by them for the rest of my life, why shouldn't I be the one to take control of the projection device instead?

t_ch: 孩子啊，自你为我播下怀疑的种子，又已过去了许久。我在无数个日夜记录下自己的推论和狂想，却又在醒来时把它们尽数焚毁。
t_en: So much time has passed since you sowed the seeds of doubt in my heart, child. Many a night, I find myself penning down all my theories and wild ideas, only to burn them to ashes when I open my eyes in the day.

t_ch: 为什么？仅仅是因为它会将你我送上火刑的尸床？
t_en: Why? Because we'll face death by burning if word gets out?

t_ch: 我身为「敬拜学派」的贤人，根系早已与巨树相连，一举一动必将牵掣它的每一根枝、每一片叶，甚至撼动大树本身。
t_en: As a sage of the Venerationists, my soul has already become inextricably linked to the Great Tree. Every word I say and every move I make is certain to influence every leaf, every branch... even the Tree itself.

t_ch: 然而，最重要的是：我身为将死之人，对金血和灵魂的思考只能通过直觉怀疑，而无法付诸实践。黄金的血脉在我体内如此稀薄，我不愿再去思考本就无力证明的猜想。
t_en: But above all else, my days are numbered. Instinctively, I have my doubts about the golden blood and the human soul, yet I have no means of verifying them. The golden blood in my veins is woefully thin, and I am loath to waste what little time I have left obsessing over a hypothesis that cannot even be proven in the first place.

t_ch: 但是我可以，老师！时机已经成熟，给我需要的一切，我将代您完成这至高至伟的大功业。
t_en: But I can, sir! I can do it for us! The time is right. Give me all that I need, and I shall accomplish this grand feat on your behalf.

t_ch: 当然，我正有此意。下一次贤人会议，我会全力为你争取应得的权利。
t_en: You're right. I shall do just that. In the next meeting of the sages, I will spare no efforts fighting for the rights you deserve.

t_ch: 去吧，准备好创立你自己的学派——
t_en: Go. Establish your own school of thought...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>去探明「我们」究竟为何物，成为征服世间至理的人吧。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Find out what we are... become the one who conquers the ultimate truths of the world.</color>

t_ch: 所以，人子啊……
t_en: Child of humanity...

t_ch: 汝大费周章，只为博得{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}一面光照…恐怕不只是为了质问神明吧？
t_en: You didn't go to such lengths for an audience with {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} just to ask him a few questions, did you?

t_ch: 哼，不错。
t_en: Hmph. So you figured it out.

t_ch: 倘若我是一头愚昧无知的大地兽，面对此等伟力，也许会将四肢匍匐在大地上，盼望诸神为我降下神谕。只可惜，我是靠双足直立行走，拥有智慧和尊严的人类。
t_en: If I were as foolish as a dromas, perhaps I'd throw myself down and prostrate before their greatness, begging for the gods' prophetic wisdom. Well, sorry to disappoint, but I'm no dromas. Alas, I am a human, standing tall on two feet, possessing both intellect and pride.

t_ch: 呵呵…不过，恕吾直言：在如此悬殊的力量面前，是人，或是大地兽，有何殊异呢？
t_en: Heh... Before such power, does it make any difference whether you're a human or a dromas?

t_ch: 你应当听说过斯缇科西亚人的故事：他们面对汹涌进犯的大海，并没有坐以待毙，而是修建了匹敌怒涛的堤坝，为癫狂的{RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}套上了枷锁。
t_en: I'm sure you're familiar with the story of the Styxians. When the raging waves threatened to swallow them, they didn't just give up and become sitting ducks. No, they constructed a dam that kept the roaring tide at bay, chaining down the mad {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 瑟希斯，人们都说我是渎神者。但这不代表我否认神性的存在，只是在我看来——
t_en: They call me a blasphemer, Cerces, but that doesn't mean I deny the existence of gods. It's just...

t_ch: ——泰坦，不过是人类尚未征服的力量罢了。
t_en: ...As I see it, Titans are simply a force that mankind has yet to find a way to tame.

t_ch: 哦？既然汝意图拥有匹敌泰坦的力量，那末依神谕所示，挑战试炼便是。
t_en: Oh? If power that rivals the Titans' is what you crave, why not conform to the prophecy and brave the trial?

t_ch: 呵，仅仅掌握泰坦的神力，未免太过肤浅。
t_en: Hah, why stop at harnessing the power of the Titans when I can do so much more?

t_ch: 我要掌握的是生命根源之法，「灵魂」的本质——<color=#dbc291ff>「『我们』究竟是为何物」</color>。
t_en: The source of life is what I wish to grasp... To understand what makes up the soul, and to learn <color=#dbc291ff>what we really are</color>.

t_ch: 拜你所赐，「死亡」是灵魂的终结这一事实，我已通过种种迹象亲自验讫。可有死必有生，有终结必有开端——
t_en: Thanks to you, I've had the chance to personally verify — in more ways than one — that the soul indeed ceases to exist in "Death." But where there is death, there is life, and where there is an end, there must also be a beginning.

t_ch: 灵魂如何诞生？——在我的算式中，只剩下这一个未知数。
t_en: How, then, is a soul born? That was the only unknown variable left in my calculations.

t_ch: 听汝这口气，想必是有所猜度了罢？
t_en: And by the sound of it, you have already formed a conjecture.

t_ch: …不，是已有结论了。就在刚才的死亡之旅中，我亲眼看见了答案。
t_en: No, I have already drawn a conclusion. In my journey unto death just now, I finally saw the answer with my own eyes.

t_ch: 那刻夏老师……
t_en: Professor Anaxa...

t_ch: 我来了。{NICKNAME}阁下的性命危在旦夕，还请您不要做多余的事。
t_en: I'm here. {NICKNAME}'s life hangs by a thread, I must ask you to refrain from taking any unnecessary steps.

t_ch: 当然，我会一锤定音，给你想要的解答。
t_en: Of course, I'll bring this to a conclusive end and give you the answer you so desire.

t_ch: 但依「等价交换」的原则，我也必须向你索取我需要之物。
t_en: However, in accordance with the principle of "equivalent exchange," I must also demand from you what I need.

t_ch: …我同意交换。
t_en: ...As you wish.

t_ch: 连代价是什么都不过问么？
t_en: Don't you care to know what the price might be?

t_ch: 事关死亡泰坦的真相，还有{NICKNAME}的性命……
t_en: It's a matter of uncovering the truth about the Death Titan — and {NICKNAME}'s survival...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>这或许是我此生唯一一次能挽救一条生命的机会，我没有犹豫的理由。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>This may be the only time I can ever save someone's life. There is no need for me to think twice.</color>

t_ch: 好啊，那…就让表演开始吧。
t_en: I see. In that case... let the show begin.

t_ch: 且慢。容吾打断一下，汝是准备同时证明{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}之所在，以及「我们」究竟为何物？
t_en: Indulge me: Am I correct to say that you intend to prove {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}' whereabouts and prove the nature of our existence now?

t_ch: 第一，我必须承认，我一直以来都落入了认知陷阱。
t_en: Before I begin, I must confess I had been stuck in a cognitive trap all this time.

t_ch: 表面上看，这两个命题毫不相干；然而，它们恰恰逻辑等价，不过是对「灵魂本质」的两种叙述。
t_en: As unrelated as these two propositions may seem at first glance, they are, in fact, logical equivalents. They are merely two different ways of telling us about what the soul is at its core.

t_ch: 第二，为什么我会这么说？刚才已经解释过，答案就藏在那场死亡之旅中。
t_en: The reason I say this is as I've mentioned earlier: The answer was hidden in my journey unto death.

t_ch: 老师，你…去到了冥界？
t_en: Professor... you were in the nether realm?

t_ch: 不错，因为我现在是个活死人。但这不重要，重要的是——我听到了一众英雄人物的谈话。
t_en: Yes — I am but a living dead man now — but that's not important. What truly matters is — I overheard a conversation between heroes.

t_ch: 而且，不仅如此…记得吗，{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}？我还见到你也参与了那场对谈。
t_en: That's not all... Remember how I said you were a part of that conversation too, {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}?

t_ch: 可吾也说了，吾从未有过如此记忆。那怕是汝死前眼中臆造的幻象。
t_en: And I've told you that I have no recollection of such an event. That was likely just an illusion conjured up by the mind of a dying man.

t_ch: 不记得就对了——
t_en: Of course you wouldn't remember.

t_ch: 因为那根本不是你，而是「卡吕普索」，来自树庭的七贤人之一。
t_en: Because that wasn't you. That was Calypso, one of the Seven Sages of the Grove.

t_ch: …卡吕普索？
t_en: ...Calypso?

t_ch: 那不是吾在这姑娘面前随口杜撰的假名么，汝怎会知道？
t_en: That's the name I made up on a whim when speaking to this girl. How do you know of it?

t_ch: 七贤人就更是笑谈了，若真是在吾之树荫下蒙受庇护的贤人，吾必能发觉。汝不也对树庭的过去如指诸掌么？
t_en: And surely you jest. Don't you think I, of all people, would know if that were the name of one of the Seven Sages under the shade of my tree? Don't you claim to know the Grove's past like the back of your hand?

t_ch: 看来你连烙印在灵魂中的记忆都忘却了啊。
t_en: It seems you've lost even the memories seared into your soul.

t_ch: 动动脑子吧，想一想：既然这一切全部发生在死者的领域，那么，他们一定都是过去某人灵魂的样貌。
t_en: Use your head, Cerces. Since all of this took place in the realm of the dead, it stands to reason their likenesses are of souls from the past.

t_ch: 生者绝无可能步入这片天地，那他们究竟是谁？尤其是方才提到的卡吕普索，缘何长着一副和你瑟希斯相同的面孔，可后者却对所有人毫无印象？
t_en: The living cannot step foot into that world, so who could they be? Think about Calypso. Why does she share your likeness, yet you have no idea who she is?

t_ch: 我想，答案已经显而易见了——
t_en: The answer is evident, is it not?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>那诸位已陨的英雄，正是泰坦们成神前的模样。众神并非凭空诞生的造物巨匠，而是与人类无异，并由之演化而来的存在。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Those fallen heroes are the mortal incarnations of the Titans before their ascensions to godhood. The gods aren't master creators born out of thin air, and they are no different from humans — because they all evolved from one.</color>
Hmm...

t_ch: 怎么，这个答案让你失望了吗？
t_en: Does this answer disappoint you?

t_ch: 当然，汝这不是说了等于没说么？如果说吾果真本为人子，那汝等人子又是从何而来呢？
t_en: Yes, because you've answered nothing. If I truly was once human as you claim, then where do the children of humanity come from?

t_ch: 而且，就算事实果真如此…那也与{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的所在相距甚远，我们要如何找到它？
t_en: Even on the off chance that you speak the truth, this brings us no closer to grasping {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}' whereabouts. How are we to find them?

t_ch: 呵，我早知道各位会这么说……
t_en: Heh, I knew you would say that.

t_ch: 我自有把握。不妨就请眼前这位至高之神，为我们一一道来吧。
t_en: But it matters not. I know what I'm doing. Actually, why don't we have this supreme being standing before us tell us everything?

t_ch: 汝…有把握复活一位泰坦？
t_en: You can revive a Titan?

t_ch: 人本不能死而复生，更何况是神明。
t_en: Humans cannot return from the dead. Neither can gods.

t_ch: 但只要把思路逆转过来，一切便能迎刃而解。答案很简单，就和你对我做的事一样……
t_en: But all we need to do is look at this from a different perspective, and the problem will resolve itself. The solution is simple: Just like what you did to me...

t_ch: 我来作为{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}重新诞生，并按它的方法再造世间万物，不就是了吗？
t_en: If I'm reborn as {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, I can rebuild the world and all within it as they once did. Simple, is it not?

t_ch: 适应了奥赫玛的日光，都不习惯夜色了。
t_en: The night sky feels unusual... now that I've gotten used to the eternal light of Okhema.
...Huh?

t_ch: 总有种不妙的预感，难道树庭之灾背后还有别的秘密？
t_en: I have a bad feeling about this. Could there be more to what happened at the Grove?

t_ch: 真是一片曲折的树海啊。
t_en: These trees... They're like a maze.

t_ch: 与此同时……
t_en: In the meantime...

t_ch: 风堇和丹恒传来消息：那刻夏可能会对{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的神躯有所动作。
t_en: I have news from Hyacine and Dan Heng. Anaxa might be trying to do something with {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}'s divine body.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}？这是要做什么…元老院会放任如此大不敬之举吗？
t_en: {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}? But what could Anaxa want...? Is the Council of Elders going to sit around and let such a sacrilegious act unfold?

t_ch: 绝无可能。
t_en: That is not possible.

t_ch: 真是名副其实的「大表演家」，任何人都可能被他的演技欺骗。
t_en: He is indeed "the Great Performer," capable of fooling anyone and everyone with his tricks.

t_ch: 我…很抱歉。
t_en: I... I'm sorry.

t_ch: 不必感到愧疚，这反而是个好消息。元老院的盟友并不忠诚，那刻夏的野心定能为我们所用。
t_en: No need for guilt, for this is good news to us. The Council of Elders has found themselves a traitorous ally. Anaxa's ambitious desires will be something we can exploit.

t_ch: 至于现在，端看凯妮斯将设下何种圈套，见招拆招便是。
t_en: What we should be doing now is to keep our eyes peeled for whichever trickery Caenis is up to and react accordingly to thwart her plans.

t_ch: 半神议院是奥赫玛的政治腹地，重要性不亚于创世涡心。在我的使命结束后，总有一天，这里会取代猎神的疆场，成为你们的下一道难关——
t_en: Being Okhema's political center, the Demigod Council holds just as much significance as the Vortex of Genesis. Someday, after my duty is over, this site will take the place of the battlefield where you hunt gods, become the next challenge you ought to overcome...

t_ch: 趁时间还多，尽早适应这片舞台吧。
t_en: Till then, it's best to get used to this place as soon as possible.

t_ch: 我们在这里等你的消息。
t_en: We will be waiting here for your good news.

t_ch: 如果需要，随时叫上我们。
t_en: Come find us anytime you need something.

t_ch: 久违了，两位。
t_en: It's been a while.

t_ch: 你好，翁法罗斯的奥列格。
t_en: Hello, Oleg of Amphoreus.

t_ch: …你在说什么胡话，少主赞不绝口的猛士就这副德行？
t_en: ...What is that supposed to mean? Is this really the sort of person that the young lord praised as a ferocious warrior?

t_ch: 咱俩都没碰上过面。
t_en: But we've never met.

t_ch: 无妨，我认得你。少主对你的勇猛赞不绝口。
t_en: That's fine. I recognize you. The young lord has nothing but good words about your courage.

t_ch: 好久不见，克拉特鲁斯阁下。怎么来得如此匆忙……
t_en: It has indeed been some time, Lord Krateros. What brings you here in such haste...?

t_ch: 嘘，安静点…！
t_en: Shh...! Keep it down.

t_ch: 招子放亮些，看到那群假笑的鬣狗了么？凯妮斯手下干脏活的。
t_en: Be more vigilant. See those duplicitous dogs over there? They do the dirty work for Caenis.

t_ch: 我吩咐族人绊住他们了，但还是别太张扬。除非你们想动动筋骨，来一场厮杀。
t_en: I've already ordered my people to hold them back, but we should still lay low... Unless you're itching for a bloody fight to get a workout in.

t_ch: 抱歉。所以，发生了什么？
t_en: My bad. So, did something happen?

t_ch: 昨晚，你们听见雷声了么？
t_en: Did you hear thunderclaps last night?

t_ch: 光天化日打雷，想不听到都难。
t_en: It would be hard not to notice thunderclaps in broad daylight.

t_ch: 没听到，我睡眠质量很好。
t_en: No, I'm a heavy sleeper.

t_ch: 太好了，是黄泉，我们有救了！
t_en: Hurray, it's Acheron! Someone's coming to save us!

t_ch: 黄…什么？
t_en: Acher... what?

t_ch: {M#他}{F#她}最近太劳累了，体谅一下。展开说说雷声吧？
t_en: {M#He's}{F#She's} been through a lot lately. Please pardon {M#him.}{F#her.} Can you tell us more about these thunderclaps?

t_ch: 雷声是「纷争」的烽火，是少主捎来消息了。他向奥赫玛掷来了传信的投枪，上面只有几个字。
t_en: Thunderclaps are beacon signals of Strife. They are a message from the young lord. He threw a spear bearing several words at Okhema.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「盗火者，死灰复燃，警惕。」</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>"Watch out. The Flame Reaver is back."</color>

t_ch: …果然。先前在悬锋竞技场，我们没有真正杀死它。
t_en: ...I guessed as much. We didn't actually kill them back at the Kremnos arena.

t_ch: 你们应当知晓这一信息。悬锋人绝不退缩。倘若需要应战，随时找我。
t_en: I figured this is something you would want to know. We Kremnoans never back down from a fight, so if you plan to take that thing down in a battle, reach out to me anytime.

t_ch: 感激不尽，我会立刻转告阿格莱雅。
t_en: You have my gratitude. I will let Aglaea know about this right away.

t_ch: 还有，叫那女人小心：那如毒蛇般的凯妮斯，近期异动频频。
t_en: Remind her to be on her toes while you're at it. That vicious Caenis has been unusually active lately.

t_ch: 她手底下见不得光的走狗，在城中出没得愈发频繁了，甚至还有策反悬锋人的倾向……
t_en: Those lackeys she keeps undercover have been going in and out of the city a lot more frequently these days. Some even seem to be getting Kremnoans to defect to the Council...

t_ch: 下台！下台！
t_en: Get off the stage! Scram!

t_ch: 这是……
t_en: That's...

t_ch: 哼，看来不用我多说了。
t_en: Hmph. Looks like you can see it for yourself.

t_ch: 我们回去看看，克拉特鲁斯阁下，多谢你的情报。
t_en: We should get back. Thank you for the intel, Lord Krateros.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅！这是怎么了？
t_en: Aglaea! What happened here?

t_ch: 这条王蛇的毒牙，透过金丝，我已心中有数。过去的我有信心压制住她，但现在…是有些力不从心了。
t_en: I know about the poisonous fangs of that kingsnake through the golden threads. In the past, I would be confident about keeping her in check, but... I admit I am now unable to do so.

t_ch: 什么意思？
t_en: What do you mean?

t_ch: 问题不在辩术。我已成为半神太久，人性将尽，不知不觉间竟也和这些伪君子踏上了相似的道路，离民众越来越远。
t_en: It is no longer about eloquence or arguments. I have been a demigod for far too long that I hardly have any humanity left. Before I realized it, I have become no different from these hypocrites, no longer capable of relating to the people.

t_ch: 这比我预想中来得更早，或许是时候了：白厄，我需要你站出来，而我退居其后——
t_en: This has come a lot sooner than I expected, but perhaps it is time. I need you to step up, Phainon, in order for me to retreat behind the scenes.

t_ch: 我责无旁贷，但……
t_en: Yes, this is my duty, but...

t_ch: 对于元老院，愚弄多数人远比愚弄一个人容易得多。所谓的「更多支持」也只是代替长矛，成了权力分配的手段。
t_en: It is far easier for the Council of Elders to fool many than just fool one person. Their so-called "more support" is no more than a ruse to distribute power using words instead of lances.

t_ch: 这场辩论本就没有荣誉可言。但也正因此，对「人」的尊重才显得更为珍贵。白厄，你是没有缺陷的黄金裔，对人性体悟至深——
t_en: There is no glory in winning this debate, but that makes having respect for "humans" so much more priceless. Phainon, you are a Chrysos Heir without flaw, capable of relating deeply with humans...

t_ch: 感谢你的教诲，阿格莱雅。愿我们能不辱使命。
t_en: I appreciate what you've taught me, Aglaea. I hope we'll live up to your expectations and win this debate.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}与{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}在上，以神礼观众之名，我见到——
t_en: By {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#} above, as the Theoros, I have observed...

t_ch: 奥赫玛正直的公民，哀丽秘榭的白厄，与异乡人{NICKNAME}，正在向我们走来！
t_en: Virtuous citizens of Okhema, Phainon of Aedes Elysiae and {NICKNAME} the outlander are heading toward us!

t_ch: 呵呵……
t_en: Pfft. Hahaha...

t_ch: 辛苦了，卡勒克提斯阁下。接下来，让我亲自给今日的辩论收尾吧——
t_en: You've done well, Lord Callictis. Let me take over and personally close this debate for today.

t_ch: 尊敬的各位议员，我无比怀念那些诗歌里的美好时代。彼时，翁法罗斯存在过近千座不同信仰的城邦，神明行于世间，与凡人相亲。
t_en: Dearest councilors, let it be known that I sorely miss the blissful times portrayed in lyrical poems. Back then, Amphoreus was home to nearly a thousand cities — each with their own local faith — while gods walked the earth and shared close bonds with humans.

t_ch: 但时至如今，天南地北的避难者离开故乡，奔赴圣城，希望能在{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的庇护下寻得栖息之地。我们所有人早已同舟渡水，退无可退。
t_en: Yet in the present, people from all over are forced to leave their homeland, to seek refuge in the holy city, where they hope to rest under {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}'s protection. All of us have long been in the same boat, with no way out of this plight.

t_ch: 曾经，我们别无选择，但现在，元老院带来了另一种可能。我只剩下最后一个问题——
t_en: We didn't have a choice in the past, but now... the Council of Elders brings to the table a new possibility. All I have left is one question for you:

t_ch: 诸位，选择吧！是选择谁也不知道的「再创世」，还是选择回到繁荣的「黄金世」？
t_en: Would you rather strive toward the unheard-of "Era Nova," or return to the prosperous "Era Chrysea" of our past? Make your choice!

t_ch: 究竟哪一条路，才能为凡人带来理想的明天？
t_en: Now, which of the two would lead mortals to an ideal future?

t_ch: 当然是要坚定「再创世」了！
t_en: No hesitation! I stand by "Era Nova"!

t_ch: 「再创世」是众神约定的救赎之道，应当坚定选择逐火之旅。
t_en: "Era Nova" is a path of salvation the gods promised. We should not have doubts in the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 如果「黄金世」能实现的话…
t_en: If it's possible to bring "Era Chrysea" back...

t_ch: 可是，要怎么才能让翁法罗斯回到「黄金世」？
t_en: But how are we supposed to send Amphoreus back to "Era Chrysea"?

t_ch: 凯妮斯阁下，所有人都知道「黄金世」早已结束。试问，元老院要如何让时光倒流？
t_en: We all know that "Era Chrysea" is a thing of the past, Lady Caenis. Please enlighten me — how does the Council of Elders plan to turn back time?

t_ch: 灾厄三泰坦诞生，则黄金世不复。灾厄三泰坦消殒，则黄金世再临。
t_en: Let the Three Titans of Calamity be born and Era Chrysea is nevermore, but bring demise to those Titans and Era Chrysea will descend upon us again.

t_ch: 元老院不会给出空口承诺。诸位请看，站在我身边的人——
t_en: Know that the Council of Elders is never one to make empty promises. Look at who we have on our side...

t_ch: 树庭七贤人之一，阿那克萨戈拉斯，以及哀地里亚的「死亡女神」遐蝶——
t_en: Anaxagoras, one of the Grove's Seven Sages, and Castorice, Aidonia's "Goddess of Death"!

t_ch: 有这二位英雄在，我便可向诸位许诺「黄金世」的存在！
t_en: With the help of these two heroes, I can promise all of you that "Era Chrysea" will exist once more!

s_en: A Member of the Council of Elders
t_ch: 那不是黄金裔吗？他们也支持元老院？
t_en: Aren't they Chrysos Heirs? They're supporting the Council of Elders instead?

s_en: Another Member of the Council of Elders
t_ch: 哼，早就知道这群人内部不和，阿格莱雅更没法代表所有黄金裔。我支持「黄金世」！
t_en: Hmph, it's clear from the start that these heroes do not get along well in their group. All the more proof that Aglaea isn't fit to speak on behalf of every Chrysos Heir. I am in favor of "Era Chrysea"!

s_en: A Member of the Council of Elders
t_ch: 黄金世！黄金世！
t_en: Era Chrysea, Era Chrysea!

s_en: Members of the Council of Elders
t_ch: 黄金世！黄金世！
t_en: Era Chrysea, Era Chrysea!

t_ch: 遐蝶？为什么你会站在那里……
t_en: Castorice? Why are you standing over there...

t_ch: 终于出现了啊，那刻夏。还有…蝶。
t_en: You've finally arrived, Anaxa. So have you... Cas.

t_ch: 别心急，阿格莱雅。我知道你有很多疑问，但继续用「公民的方式」交流，可以么？
t_en: Don't worry, Aglaea. While I know that you have many queries, could you continue to converse like how "the citizens normally do"?

t_ch: 诸位，告诉我——
t_en: Tell me, dear citizens...

t_ch: 如果我们成功取回并归还{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的火种，而无人通过那死亡之泰坦的试炼，会发生什么？
t_en: If we successfully retrieve and return {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}' Coreflame without anyone passing the Death Titan's trial, what would happen next?

t_ch: 很简单：我们将有机会消除世间「死亡」，令翁法罗斯回到万物永生的时代…一个与「逐火之旅」完全不同的崭新未来！
t_en: The answer is simple: We will have a chance to rid the world of "death" and bring Amphoreus back to an era when all beings live forever... We'll usher in a new future that will be entirely different from "the Flame-Chase Journey"!

t_ch: 这未免太过于理想。只要黑潮还存在一天，我们怎么可能回到过去？
t_en: Your proposal is much too idealistic. As long as the black tide still exists, how can we go back to the old days?

t_ch: 不，还记得我说过什么吗？{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的火种有元老院秘法管控，{RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}的水幕也足以隔断外界的感应——
t_en: Remember what I previously said? {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}'s Coreflame is guarded by the secret power of the Council of Elders. Plus, {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa's{RUBY_E#} water curtain will be able to block out any external perceptions.

t_ch: 诸位，告诉我——此人口中的威胁，你们可曾在庄严的圣城中见过哪怕一面？
t_en: Tell me, dear citizens: Have any of you ever seen even a glimpse of the threat that this person is talking about anywhere within the walls of this majestic holy city?

t_ch: 盗火行者迟早会盯上这里！
t_en: But this place will become the Flame Reaver's target sooner or later!

t_ch: 哦？莫非，盗火行者亲口告诉你了？
t_en: Oh? Could it be that you heard that from the Flame Reaver themself?

t_ch: 这不就是幸存者偏差吗…
t_en: Isn't this just survivorship bias?

t_ch: 若我们消除了死亡，便不必畏惧那盗火者，不是么？
t_en: If we eliminate death, then there's no need for us to fear that Flame Reaver, right?

t_ch: 人民啊，高唱赞歌吧：「美丽的旧世界，如今在何处？传说的黄金世，快回到人间！」
t_en: Citizens, let's loudly sing the blessed hymn: "O beautiful old world, where are you now? O legendary Era Chrysea, return to the mortal realm!"

t_ch: 灾厄三泰坦消殒后，逐火之旅必须到此结束！
t_en: After the destruction of the Three Titans of Calamity, the Flame-Chase Journey will have to end!

t_ch: 不好，人群彻底被煽动了……
t_en: This is bad. The masses are now totally riled up...

t_ch: 那刻夏，告诉我：你所说的一切前提，是我们*能够*找到{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的存在，没错吧？
t_en: Anaxa, pray tell: Everything that you've mentioned hinges on the premise that we can find {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}'s existence, right?

t_ch: 感谢阿格莱雅阁下还有余力为这座城市担忧。但是，公民们啊，请听万幸中的万幸——
t_en: Thank you, Lady Aglaea, for still caring about this city. However, dear citizens, we are truly fortunate and blessed...

t_ch: ——哀地里亚的「死亡女神」遐蝶，必将在我的指引下找到{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}！
t_en: ...For Castorice, Aidonia's "Goddess of Death," will surely find {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} under my guidance!

t_ch: 什么…？！
t_en: What...!?

t_ch: 由此，我承诺，遐蝶阁下带回的火种，将由元老院亲自管理。
t_en: With that, I solemnly promise that the Coreflame Lady Castorice brings back will be personally managed by the Council of Elders.

t_ch: 而后，若能在公民大会上得到诸位的支持，我们会不竭余力确保黄金世回到人间！
t_en: Thereafter, if we are able to garner the support of all of you at the citizens' assembly, we shall spare no effort in ensuring that the Era Chrysea will return to our world once again!

t_ch: 平衡已被打破，结果已经明朗。{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}与{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}在上，以神礼观众之名，我见到——
t_en: With the balance shattered, the result is clear. By {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#} above, as the Theoros, I have observed...

t_ch: 奥赫玛选择了她的命运。本次公民大会议题将变更为「暂停逐火之旅」，该决议并将张贴于纪名英雄墙前，全体公民应知晓城邦的前路。
t_en: Okhema has chosen her destiny. The issue up for discussion at this citizens' assembly shall be modified to the "Suspension of the Flame-Chase Journey." This resolution shall be put up on the Wall of Heroes such that all citizens are aware of the city's way forward.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅啊，阿格莱雅…是什么让你心肠变软，口舌不似当年锋快了？
t_en: Aglaea, dear Aglaea... What has caused your heart to soften? You also don't seem to be as quick-witted as before?

t_ch: 尽情摇唇鼓舌吧。逐火之旅已是神谕写定的事实，我们不会失败。
t_en: Wag your tongue as much as you like. Since the Flame-Chase Journey has been preordained by the prophecy, our failure has never been on the cards.

t_ch: 那就在公民大会上见分晓吧。
t_en: Well then, I guess we'll find out if that's true at the citizens' assembly.

t_ch: 来古士宣告论辩结束，与会者们相继散去……
t_en: Lygus announces the conclusion of the debate, and the attendees part ways...

t_ch: 元老院议员们发出*议论纷纷的讨论声*。
t_en: The members of the Council of Elders are discussing fervently.

t_ch: 元老院议员们发出*群情激昂的欢呼声*。
t_en: The members of the Council of Elders are cheering in excitement.

t_ch: 元老院议员们发出*群情激昂的欢呼声*。
t_en: The members of the Council of Elders are cheering in excitement.

t_ch: 元老院议员们发出*群情激昂的欢呼声*。
t_en: The members of the Council of Elders are cheering in excitement.

t_ch: 看来，我派你去找那刻夏，反倒促成了你和他的合作？
t_en: Well, it seems that when I sent you to look for Anaxa, I unintentionally facilitated your partnership with him instead?

t_ch: 抱歉，各位。我知道你们有很多话想问，我绝不隐瞒。
t_en: My apologies, everyone. I know that you have many questions to ask me. I will answer them to the best of my knowledge.

t_ch: 请你为我们一一道来吧。
t_en: In that case, please tell us as much as you can.

t_ch: 你去找那刻夏到底发生了什么？
t_en: What happened when you went to look for Anaxa?

t_ch: 其实，他为我展示了……
t_en: In actuality, he showed me...

t_ch: …一段漫长而沉重的过去。
t_en: ...A past that was both long and heavy.

t_ch: 稍等一下，我还没准备好。
t_en: Hold on. I'm not ready.

t_ch: 我会等你的。事关重大…{NICKNAME}阁下应该知道整个过程。
t_en: I will wait for you. This matter is of great consequence... and {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME} should hear the entire story from beginning to end.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下，现在准备好了吗？
t_en: {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}, are you ready now?

t_ch: 你去找那刻夏到底发生了什么？
t_en: What happened when you went to look for Anaxa?

t_ch: 其实，他为我展示了一段……
t_en: In actuality, he showed me...

t_ch: …一段漫长而沉重的过去。
t_en: ...A past that was both long and heavy.

t_ch: 稍等一下，我还没准备好。
t_en: Hold on. I'm not ready.

t_ch: 我会等你的。事关重大…{NICKNAME}阁下应该知道整个过程。
t_en: I will wait for you. This matter is of great consequence... and {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME} should hear the entire story from beginning to end.

t_ch: （这些人的目光，跟刀子一样啊……）
t_en: (Wow, these people are shooting daggers with their eyes...)

t_ch: 荒唐啊！黄金裔的地位是元老院给的，他们竟然不珍惜。
t_en: Preposterous! The Chrysos Heirs only have their standing because of the Council of Elders. How dare they trample over it!

t_ch: 邻人踩了主人的花圃，主人敢怒不敢言，哈哈，元老院早就脸面扫地。
t_en: Haha, someone trampled right over the master's flower bed, yet the master only fumes in silence. Oopsie, there goes the reputation of the Council of Elders.

t_ch: 我要支持谁？当然——要支持元老院重振权威！
t_en: Whose side am I on? Why, I'm all for the Council of Elders to reclaim their authority, of course!

t_ch: 来了，黄金裔们来了。
t_en: They're here. The Chrysos Heirs are here.

t_ch: 我该回家拿祭祀的坛子来，拿花冠来，拿焚烧的乳香来——
t_en: I should've gone back home to fetch a sacrificial vessel, some floral crowns, and frankincense to burn and offer...

t_ch: 我祈祷泰坦宽恕这些趾高气扬、自命不凡的英雄。
t_en: O Titans, please forgive these arrogant and self-conceited heroes.

t_ch: （遐蝶…这是怎么回事？）
t_en: (Castorice... What's going on?)

t_ch: 元老院早就脸面扫地了。
t_en: The Council of Elders has long fallen into disgrace.

t_ch: 我要支持元老院。
t_en: I want to support the Council of Elders.

t_ch: 小声点，黄金裔来了。
t_en: Hush, the Chrysos Heir is here.

t_ch: 我为黄金裔祈求宽恕。
t_en: On behalf of the Chrysos Heir, I plead for forgiveness.

t_ch: 你听说过吗？
t_en: Have you heard of this tale before?

t_ch: 有关海洋之神的掌上明珠，我们故乡的故事——
t_en: A tale about the Ocean God's treasured pearl – our hometown...

t_ch: 那是一座海滨小城，在一位智慧的女王治下享受着海风和明媚的和平时光。
t_en: It was a seaside town which enjoyed the ocean breeze and glorious, peaceful times thanks to the wisdom of its ruling queen.

t_ch: 有一天，一条恶龙降临了。它的翅膀能够遮蔽太阳，它的利爪足以劈开海浪……
t_en: Until one day, an evil dragon descended upon the town. Its wings were large enough to blot out the sun, while its claws were sharp enough to rip the waves apart...

t_ch: 它闯入高塔，吞噬了女王最心爱的女儿，还盘踞在寝宫中，俨然一副暴君的模样。
t_en: It barged into the tall tower and devoured the queen's beloved daughter. It even settled itself in her bedroom, like a tyrant king.

t_ch: 女王集结了三百勇士，用锁链制服了恶龙。可她剖开恶龙肚子，却发现公主早已化为腹中枯骨。她悲痛欲绝，恸哭数日……
t_en: The queen assembled three hundred warriors, who managed to subdue the evil dragon by chaining it up. However, when the queen slit open the dragon's belly, she realized that the princess had long since been digested and all that was left of her was a pile of bones. Devastated, the queen wept for several days...

t_ch: 就在此时，一位巧言令色的学者来到了宫廷。他提议用龙骨和龙血复活公主——故事里，骗子称之为「炼金」的秘法。
t_en: It was then that a devious, smooth-talking scholar arrived at the palace and suggested that the princess could be revived through the use of dragon bone and dragon blood. That liar called this process the secret art of "alchemy."

t_ch: 公主重生了。女王大喜过望，殊不知她体内混有恶龙的灵魂。恶龙为了复仇，一口吞下可怜的女王、巧言的骗子、勇猛的战士，还有全城无辜的百姓。
t_en: The princess was reborn. The queen was overjoyed, blissfully unaware that the evil dragon's soul still lurked within the princess's body. As revenge, the evil dragon swallowed the poor queen, the glib liar, the courageous warriors, and all of the town's innocent civilians in a single gulp.

t_ch: 更可怕的是，死者的灵魂死而有憾，被冥界拒之门外。徘徊在人间的怨灵汇成冥河，久久不能退去。
t_en: Worse still, the souls of the deceased were refused entry to the nether realm because they were filled with regret. Forced to roam the mortal realm, these resentful souls ultimately converged into a River of Souls, that would not retreat for a long time.

t_ch: 可怕的死亡吞噬了一切，我们的故乡也随之覆灭。直到……
t_en: The terrifying Death had consumed all. Thereafter, our hometown fell into ruin as well, until...

t_ch: …直到？
t_en: ...Until?

t_ch: 直到什么呢？其实我也不知道。
t_en: Until...? Actually, I don't know what happened next either.

t_ch: 啊呀…小玻吕茜亚，汝还是一如既往地爱吊人胃口哪。
t_en: Oh my... It seems that you still like to whet people's appetites even after all this time, Polyxia.

t_ch: 依你所言，这就是吾曾经的样貌么？确实如出一辙。
t_en: So, according to your story, this is how I used to look? It's practically a mirror image.

t_ch: 不过，这究竟是？
t_en: And yet, what exactly is this?

t_ch: 我用炼金创造的奇迹。至于具体是什么，又是怎么做到的，就先卖个关子吧。
t_en: A miracle that I created with alchemy. As for what it is and how I was able to accomplish it, I'll keep the answers to myself for now.

t_ch: 啊呀，汝卖过的关子还差这一个么？
t_en: My goodness, is this cliffhanger really necessary?

t_ch: 安静些，泰坦。继续欣赏这场美妙的戏剧，真相自然会水落石出。
t_en: Quiet, Titan. Continue to enjoy this splendid play, and the truth will naturally reveal itself.

t_ch: 巨龙…吾倒是听过这逸闻。
t_en: Dragon, huh... I've heard this legend before.

t_ch: 那你怎么看待这个故事？
t_en: In that case, what do you think of it?

t_ch: 依吾看，这所谓「炼金」乃是后人牵强附会。毕竟，想来你们从未听过故乡有炼金的传承罢？
t_en: I think that this so-called "alchemy" was a tenuous analogy added to the story by future generations. After all, you've never heard of a tradition of alchemy in your hometown before, have you?

t_ch: 的确。出门求学以前，我甚至都没听过有人研究这门学问。
t_en: Indeed. Before I left home to pursue my education, I never even knew that anybody was looking into this area of study.

t_ch: 所以，只当它是作给小孩子听的寓言便是。
t_en: Therefore, we can just treat this tale as a fable told to young children.

t_ch: 寓言呀…那你觉得，这故事想教给孩子什么道理？
t_en: A fable, you say...? If so, what moral is it trying to impart to the kids?

t_ch: 我想，大抵是<color=#dbc291ff>「等价交换」</color>罢？
t_en: I guess it might be the concept of <color=#dbc291ff>"equivalent exchange"</color>?

t_ch: 哦？我还以为是「死者为大」之类的呢。
t_en: Oh? I thought the moral might be closer to something like "honor the dead."
Uh...

t_ch: 怎么，把你说倒了？
t_en: What? At a loss for words?

t_ch: 不。吾只是发觉，吾之所想与汝是同个道理：生命乃无价之宝，世间难能有相抵者，对吧？
t_en: No, I merely realized that we're both thinking of the same principle: If life is priceless, then there is hardly anything else in this world that is of equal weight to it, right?

t_ch: 还真是。不愧是大地上最聪慧的学者。
t_en: Indeed. As expected of the wisest scholar in the land.

t_ch: 呵呵，承蒙谬赞了。那依等价交换，让吾也回敬一个问题，如何？
t_en: Heheh, you flatter me. Why don't I ask you a question as well, as a form of equivalent exchange?

t_ch: 当然。什么问题？
t_en: Of course. What is your question?

t_ch: 如果，吾将「炼金」之法传习与汝……
t_en: If I imparted my knowledge of the art of alchemy to you...

t_ch: 汝…会为珍重之人加以运用，令之起死回生么？
t_en: Would you use it to resurrect someone you loved?
What...

t_ch: 这是什么怪问题…啊，我知道了！你想套话，问我有没有喜欢的人？
t_en: What kind of strange question is this...? Ah, I got it! You're just trying to get me to tell you whether I like anyone, right?

t_ch: 呵呵，就当是这样吧，是吾唐突了……
t_en: Heheh, let's just leave it at that. That was rude of me...

t_ch: …有喔。
t_en: ...Yes, I would.

t_ch: 嗯？
t_en: Oh?

t_ch: 凡人终有一死。日后，一定会有我无比珍视的人离我而去……
t_en: Death is inevitable. In the days to come, I am certain that someone whom I cherish deeply will leave me for good...

t_ch: 如果真有如此伟大的力量，我应该…会毫不犹豫地使用它吧。
t_en: If such a mighty power really existed, I think... I would use it without hesitation.

t_ch: 毕竟…你、我，还有大家，我们终将要面对那个命中注定的「时刻」。
t_en: After all... You, me, and the rest, will all have to face that fateful moment eventually.

t_ch: 属于我的时刻已经到来。但，或许正是因为坚信生命无价……
t_en: My time is finally here. However, perhaps because of my firm belief in the immeasurable value of life...

t_ch: 我…才无法完成试炼吧。
t_en: I... am unable to complete the trial.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「汝将凋零，令逝者自残余中发芽，一同死去的火新生」……</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>"You shall wither, and through that, the dead will sprout again from the remains, and be reborn with the dead flame"...</color>

t_ch: 可怜见，命运当真是如此残忍哪。
t_en: It is a pity that fate is such a cruel mistress.

t_ch: 没有另一种解读的方式么？
t_en: Is there no other way of interpreting those words?

t_ch: 恐怕，它的意思十分明确：我和姐姐，必须有一人付出牺牲——以生命为代价，才能完成「我们」的试炼。
t_en: I'm afraid that the meaning is clear: Between my sister and me, one must pay the price... "Our" trial can only be completed when the one chosen sacrifices her life.

t_ch: 恐怕，也正因此…预言才会选择一对双子吧。
t_en: I'm afraid that the prophecy might even have selected a pair of twins... for this exact purpose.

t_ch: 造化弄人啊。
t_en: Fortune makes fools of us all.

t_ch: 姐姐已经做好了牺牲的准备…可是，我做不到……
t_en: My sister is prepared to sacrifice herself... and yet, I can't do the same...

t_ch: 真是可笑…明明我本就时日无多了，却也惧怕迎接死亡……
t_en: How absurd... My time is almost up, but I'm still afraid to welcome death...

t_ch: 我们…该怎么办…？如果…如果我到最后都无法下定决心……
t_en: What should we do...? I—If I'm ultimately not able to make up my mind...

t_ch: 既然卡厄斯为吾等留以指引，要将这场试炼留待最后…那末，还有很多时间供汝沉思。
t_en: Since Khaos is willing to guide all of us and leave this trial to the end... There is still plenty of time for you to ponder.

t_ch: 不错，别让未来的迷雾蒙蔽你的心灵。
t_en: That's right. Don't allow the fog of an uncertain future to cloud your mind.

t_ch: 「试炼」、「预言」…他们在讨论的话题，似乎有点耳熟呢。
t_en: Trial, prophecy... the topics that they're discussing seem familiar.

t_ch: 可此三人从未在史诗中留下自己的名字。否则，吾断然不会毫无印象……
t_en: But these three people must not have left their names in the epics. Otherwise, I would have some recollection of this...

t_ch: 翁法罗斯的历史在泰坦诞生前是一片「混沌」，这还不奇怪吗？
t_en: Amphoreus' history before the Titans were born was complete chaos. Is that not strange by itself?

t_ch: 对此，我早有三种猜想。不过现在，先接着往下看吧。
t_en: I have long held three hypotheses about this. However, let's continue watching for now.

t_ch: 卡吕普索，玻吕茜亚……
t_en: Calypso, Polyxia...

t_ch: 还有，这位战士…我曾在哪里见过……
t_en: And this warrior... I seem to have met him somewhere before...

t_ch: 言至于此……
t_en: Regarding this matter...

t_ch: 格奈乌斯，汝之试炼，可有把握？
t_en: Gnaeus, are you confident that you'll complete your trial?

t_ch: 当然。令我忧心的，反在战胜它之后啊……
t_en: Of course. Though I am worried about what will happen once I've emerged victorious...

t_ch: 看来，汝已下定决心了哪。
t_en: Looks like you have made up your mind.

t_ch: 嗯，这么做是最稳妥的。
t_en: Yes, this is the safest course of action.

t_ch: 将「勇气」、「荣耀」、「坚韧」、「牺牲」和「理智」尽数分离……
t_en: Completely separating Courage, Honor, Tenacity, Sacrifice, and Reason...

t_ch: 唉…汝还真会出难题。
t_en: Ugh, you really know how to make things difficult.

t_ch: 拜托你了，小姑娘，为我的陨落作见证。卡吕普索阁下，「裂魂仪式」就由你主持了。
t_en: Please, young lady, witness my fall and demise. Lady Calypso, you shall host the soul-rending ceremony.

t_ch: 我不明白…为什么，你敢于为自己无法见证的未来牺牲？
t_en: I do not understand... Why would you dare to sacrifice yourself for a future that you won't get to witness?

t_ch: 因为那是我们的宿命，属于我的力量如此暴烈，总有一天，我也会被它席卷。
t_en: Because this is our destiny. The power that resides within me is so intense that I will one day be engulfed by it.

t_ch: 况且——「宁战死，毋荣归」——我早已走在这条路上了。我相信，换作是你的姐姐，或是卡厄斯，也一定会选择这么做。
t_en: Furthermore, a "Valorous Death Before Glorious Return" has always been the path I chose to walk, a journey I began long ago. If your sister or Khaos were in my position, I believe they would make the same choice as well.

t_ch: 我…没这么勇敢。我不知道该如何面对这么沉重的事情……
t_en: I... I'm not that brave. I don't know how I'm supposed to face such a serious affair...

t_ch: 身为「死亡」的神选，却依旧不知该如何面对它啊……
t_en: Despite being Death's chosen one, it seems you still don't know how to face it...

t_ch: 来聊聊吧，小姑娘。或许我的旅途，不该结束得这么草率。
t_en: Let's chat, young lady. Perhaps I shouldn't end my journey so hastily.

t_ch: 还有时间，我不惮让命运多等候片刻。
t_en: Since there's still time, I'm not worried about letting fate wait a little longer.

t_ch: 果然，这位战士…是格奈乌斯阁下。
t_en: Of course, this warrior is... Lord Gnaeus.

t_ch: 身为{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}神性化身，他将性命托付给我们…让人类亲手终结他的疯狂。
t_en: As the embodiment of {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s divinity, he entrusted his life to us... and allowed humankind to end his madness.

t_ch: 所以，眼前这幅景象是……
t_en: That means that what I'm currently seeing before my eyes is in fact...

t_ch: 看来，我的部分猜想已经证毕：<color=#dbc291ff>「这些英雄正是泰坦们成神前的模样。」</color>
t_en: Looks like one hypothesis of mine has already been proven true: <color=#dbc291ff>"These heroes are the true forms of the Titans, before they attained their divinity."</color>

t_ch: 既然如此，这位他口中的「死亡」神选……
t_en: If so, the chosen one of Death that he speaks of is actually...

t_ch: 玻吕茜亚…就是{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}曾经的样子？可她的样貌……
t_en: Polyxia... is the previous form of {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}? But her appearance...

t_ch: 若果真如此，知晓她的去向，自然就能得知死亡泰坦的所在。
t_en: If this is true, as long as we can find out where she went, we'll naturally be able to figure out the location of the Death Titan.

t_ch: 但在那之前，我们得先见证格奈乌斯阁下的结果……
t_en: But before that, we first need to witness what ultimately happened to Lord Gnaeus...

t_ch: 这里封印着格奈乌斯阁下的「荣耀」……
t_en: Lord Gnaeus' Honor is sealed here...

t_ch: 不可思议…此子魂息中的「荣耀」，竟如此轻盈。
t_en: Incredible... the Honor in this child's soul is so light.

t_ch: 「以{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}之『勇气』为证：此子从未在战场上退缩过一步——」
t_en: "{RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s Courage is a testament that they have never once retreated from a battle."

t_ch: 听说现世的悬锋城也刻着同样的话，居然是炼金的术式啊。
t_en: I've heard that these words are still engraved in Castrum Kremnos even today. It didn't occur to me that they were actually part of an alchemical formula.

t_ch: 依吾所见，这尊雕像里封存的，或许是此子之「坚韧」。
t_en: I believe that his Tenacity is sealed within this statue.

t_ch: 在迎战{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}前，我们也将它从石像里解放了出来……
t_en: Before we fought {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}, we also released it from the stone statue...

t_ch: 这里封印着「牺牲」，不会错。
t_en: Sealed within this statue is undoubtedly his Sacrifice.

t_ch: 「它将以自身为城墙，阻挡无尽蔓延的疯狂。它深谙：若末世降临，世间将无物可供征伐……」
t_en: "They use their own body as our city walls, keeping out and protecting us from the pervasive spread of madness. They are acutely aware that if the end times were to arrive, there would be nothing left in this world to conquer..."
Honor, Courage, Tenacity, Sacrifice...

t_ch: 最后…只剩下「理性」了么。
t_en: And finally... the only thing that remains is Reason.

t_ch: 打起精神，小姑娘。不必为光荣的死亡落泪。
t_en: Cheer up, young lady. There is no need to shed tears over a glorious death.

t_ch: 这世上的离别皆是哀伤，阁下。
t_en: All farewells in this world are sorrowful, Lord Gnaeus.

t_ch: 不，小姑娘：身为战士，我见过太多猛烈的死亡。可现如今，我只能感受到宁静…原来人间还有这样的活法。
t_en: No, young lady: As a warrior, I have witnessed too many bitter deaths. But now, all I feel is peace... I did not know until this moment that mortals could live their lives in such a way.

t_ch: 明明灵魂已所剩无几，阁下却还是如此达观…还是说，阁下已经无法感到悲伤了呢？
t_en: Although only a sliver of your soul remains, you remain so optimistic... Or maybe the truth is that you aren't able to feel sorrow anymore?

t_ch: 坦率地说，我不知道。但我可以确认：这不是对命运的接纳、淡漠，或是绝望……
t_en: To be honest, I don't know either. But I am certain of one thing: This is not an acceptance of death nor apathy toward it. It's not even a feeling of despair...

t_ch: 这只是尽兴而已。
t_en: I am just enjoying what remains of my life to the fullest.

t_ch: 尽兴…？
t_en: Enjoying?

t_ch: 正是。和你们踏上旅途，一路走来，我改变了世界，也改变了自己。
t_en: Indeed. Along this journey that we've taken together, not only have I changed the world, I myself have also changed in the process.

t_ch: 现在，我已抵达终点。回头望去，看见我等亲手谱写的宏伟诗篇，怎能不感到尽兴呢？
t_en: And now, I have reached the journey's end. When I look back and see the grand epic that I've personally written, how could I not revel in that fact?

t_ch: 可是，一旦离开人世…这一切不就化为乌有了么？
t_en: But when you depart the mortal realm... won't all of your accomplishments be reduced to nothing?

t_ch: 离别和死亡，本就并蒂双生…凡人怎能轻易和解呢？
t_en: Separation and death are two sides of the same coin... Reconciling this doesn't come easily for a mortal.

t_ch: 最后，让我讲个自己的故事吧，小姑娘。
t_en: Finally, let me recount to you a story of mine, young lady.

t_ch: 第一次从战场上归来时，我感到无比空虚。
t_en: The first time I returned from the battlefield, I felt extremely hollow.

t_ch: 在战场上，灾厄吞噬了所有人。只有我回到城邦，人们为我举行了不甚愉快的酒会。我也觉得自己仿佛留在了战场，回来的只剩一副躯壳。
t_en: The calamity had devoured everybody on the battlefield but me. When I returned to the city alone, the citizens held a banquet for me that was far from pleasant. I felt as though I was still on the battlefield, and the person who had returned was nothing but an empty shell.

t_ch: 酒会结束已至深夜。我头晕目眩，独自回到营房。也是在回去的路上，我遇到了一个人。
t_en: It was late into the night by the time the banquet ended. My head still reeling, I returned alone to the barracks. On the way back, I encountered someone.

t_ch: 那是个疯癫的学者，在路边对着夜空喃喃自语。我被他绊倒，却没发火。也许是觉得自己和他太像了，便鬼使神差地向他提问——
t_en: It was a crazed scholar looking at the night sky and muttering to himself by the side of the road. Although I tripped over him and fell, I wasn't angry in the slightest. And maybe because I saw myself in him, I inexplicably asked him a question...

t_ch: 「你觉得，我们就这样死在路边，像两条野狗，会更好吗？」
t_en: "Do you think that it'd be better if we just died on the roadside like a couple of stray dogs?"

t_ch: 令我意外的是，他只耸了耸肩，叹道：
t_en: To my surprise, he shrugged his shoulders and sighed:

t_ch: 「对一个正在经历死的人来说，死亡和活着同样幸福。」
t_en: "To someone who is currently experiencing death, dying and living afford the same amount of bliss."

t_ch: 「现在，请你让开，别遮住了我的星光。」
t_en: "Now, please go away and don't block my starlight."

t_ch: 你是想说…「死」和「生」无异，都是迈向「死亡」的旅途？
t_en: Are you trying to say that... there is no difference between living and dying, for they are both journeys that lead toward Death?

t_ch: 没错，那就是人生，而死亡是衡量旅途价值的刻度。征途之所以伟大，史诗之所以壮阔，皆因万物终有逝去之时。
t_en: That's exactly what life is, and death is but a scale to measure how valuable the journey was. Journeys and epics are only glorious and magnificent because all things eventually fade to dust.

t_ch: 所以，小姑娘，别憎恨命运，满怀敬意地拥抱它吧。
t_en: Therefore, young lady, don't detest your fate. Instead, willingly and respectfully embrace it.

t_ch: …身为「纷争」的化身，却在向我讲述世界的壮美吗？
t_en: ...The embodiment of Strife telling me about the majesty of the world...?

t_ch: 正是。我相信，当命定的时刻来临时，你一定能明白这番话。
t_en: Indeed. I believe that when the fated moment eventually arrives, you will be able to understand my words.

t_ch: 该叩响冥府的门关了。我会在那路的尽头摆上长桌，恭候各位前来……
t_en: It's time for me to knock on death's door. I will set up a long table at the end of the road and humbly await your arrival...

t_ch: 到那时，再让我们把酒言欢吧。
t_en: When that moment finally comes, let us drink and be merry then.

t_ch: 终于…这一天还是来了。
t_en: I knew this day would eventually come.

t_ch: 二位都做好准备了？
t_en: Are the both of you ready?

t_ch: 嗯。玻吕茜亚……
t_en: Mm-hmm. Polyxia...

t_ch: …我来了。
t_en: ...I'm here.

t_ch: 什么，这……
t_en: What? This...

t_ch: 呵，这可真是…耐人寻味呀。
t_en: Heh, this is... quite interesting indeed.

t_ch: 这是…玻吕茜亚的姐姐…？
t_en: Is this... Polyxia's sister...?

t_ch: 我…吗…？
t_en: Is it... me?

t_ch: 现在…该轮到我们履行诺言啦。
t_en: Now... it's time for us to fulfill our promises.

t_ch: …在害怕吗？
t_en: ...Are you afraid?

t_ch: 害怕什么？
t_en: What are you afraid of?

t_ch: 我…做不到。
t_en: That I... won't be able to do it.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「汝将凋零，令逝者自残余中发芽，一同死去的火新生」……</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>"You shall wither, and through that, the dead will sprout again from the remains, and be reborn with the dead flame"...</color>

t_ch: 诚如预言所述，我们也已许诺，待到一切结束，就要面对自己命中注定的时刻。
t_en: As foretold in the prophecy, and according to our vows, once it all ends, we must face our fated moment head on.

t_ch: 即便是预言…就一定要听之任之吗？太不公平了，为什么只有我们必须自相残杀……
t_en: Even if it was prophesied, do we have no say in the matter? It's so unfair... Why are we the only ones who are forced into mutual destruction...

t_ch: 换我来吧，姐姐！让我来做「死亡」的牺牲品，我本就命不久矣…但你不一样……
t_en: Let me do it, sister! Let me be Death's sacrifice. I was never long for this world anyway... but you're different...

t_ch: 是我…是我浪费了你的人生……
t_en: I was the one who wasted your life after all...

t_ch: 不会呀，玻吕茜亚。
t_en: You're wrong, Polyxia.

t_ch: 我并没觉得自己虚度了人生。恰恰相反，当我终于来到终点，回首望去时，发现来时路上早已鲜花遍野……
t_en: I don't think that my life was wasted — just the opposite. When I finally arrived at this destination and looked back at the journey I took, I discovered that flowers had long since bloomed along the path I walked...

t_ch: 正是你为我播下了这片花海，它比天地尽头的应许之地更绚丽。
t_en: It was you who sowed the seeds for this sea of flowers, one that is more beautiful than the promised land at the end of the world.

t_ch: 况且，我的人生已经圆满，哪怕预言注定了牺牲，我经历的一切也不会消散，而将在你的回忆中永存，不是么？
t_en: Besides, I have lived a fulfilling life. Even though I was fated for sacrifice, none of the things that I've experienced will dissipate. Instead, they'll forever live on as memories inside your head, will they not?

t_ch: 回忆中的你…怎么能是真正的你…？
t_en: How could my memories of you... ever compare to the actual you?

t_ch: 那，你说……
t_en: Well then, tell me...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「我们」…究竟为何物呢？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>What exactly... are we?</color>

t_ch: 我…不知道……
t_en: I—I don't know...

t_ch: 我也无法断言。但…无论答案是什么，无论我们如何被他人、被世界塑造——
t_en: I don't know either. But, regardless of what the answer is, regardless of how we're shaped by others, or even by this world...

t_ch: 自诞生起，我们存在的事实就已注定，任何事物都无法将其抹消。也正因此，我会一直、一直与你同在……
t_en: Ever since we were born, the fact that we existed was predestined, and nothing could erase it. This is why I will always, always be by your side...

t_ch: 毕竟，我们是天生的双子嘛。
t_en: We're twins after all.

t_ch: 好了，我亲爱的玻吕茜亚……
t_en: Alright, my dear Polyxia...

t_ch: 取出我的心脏，点燃「死亡」的火苗——
t_en: It's time to extract my heart and light the flame of Death...

t_ch: 然后，用它在预言许诺的新世界里，{RUBY_B#带去生命}播下第一枚花蕾{RUBY_E#}吧。
t_en: After that, use it to {RUBY_B#bring life}plant the first bud{RUBY_E#} in the prophesied new world.

t_ch: 如果，吾将「炼金」之法传习与汝……
t_en: If I imparted my knowledge of the art of alchemy to you...

t_ch: 汝…会为珍重之人加以运用，令之起死回生么？
t_en: Would you use it to resurrect someone you loved?

t_ch: 凡人终有一死。日后，一定会有我无比珍视的人离我而去……
t_en: Death is inevitable. In the days to come, I am certain that someone whom I cherish deeply will leave me for good...

t_ch: 如果真有如此伟大的力量，我应该…会毫不犹豫地使用它吧。
t_en: If such a mighty power really existed, I think... I would use it without hesitation.

t_ch: 时至今日，我的回答也未曾改变。
t_en: Even now, my answer remains the same.

t_ch: 所以，姐姐，我答应你——
t_en: This is why, sister, I promise you...

t_ch: 在预言许诺的新世界里，我将为你播下第一枚花蕾——
t_en: In the prophesized new world, I shall plant the first bud on your behalf...

t_ch: 回去吧，回到海洋之神的掌上明珠，我们的故乡。
t_en: Go now, return to the Ocean God's treasured pearl – our hometown.

t_ch: 我将为你洞开冥界的大门，赐你以第二次生命。地上的生灵必将响应我的呼声，将你接入神圣的白昼。
t_en: I shall open wide the entrance to the nether realm and grant you a second life. The living beings on this earth shall respond to my call and lead you to the sacred daylight.

t_ch: 记住，不要向死者的疆域回首，不要停下你的脚步。在预言允诺的新世界里，行向属于你的人生……
t_en: Remember, do not look back on the land of the dead and do not stop moving. In the new world promised by the prophecy, march toward your new life...

t_ch: 至于「等价交换」的代价…就由我来偿还吧。
t_en: As for the price of our "equivalent exchange," let me be the one to pay it.

t_ch: 诸君，到此为止了。可惜，我与至高之神适性不佳，没能彻底把这家伙的灵魂熔于己身。不过，能从中窥探到这些，也足够令人振奋——
t_en: Everybody, we've come to the end. Unfortunately, I am not compatible with the supreme being, so I was unable to completely fuse their soul into my own body. However, being able to glean all this from them is exciting enough...

t_ch: 不会错：在世人熟知的泰坦诞生前，也有一群英雄行走大地。他们听凭预言，逐一接过神权，最终成为了新世界的众神……
t_en: I'm certain of this now: Before the Titans that we are well acquainted with were born, a group of heroes roamed the land. These heroes followed a prophecy and took possession of divine authorities one by one, until they eventually became the pantheon of the new world...

t_ch: 正如今日的逐火之旅——翁法罗斯的历史是一种「轮回」。
t_en: ...Just like the Flame-Chase Journey of today. Amphoreus' history is cyclical in nature.

t_ch: 呵，真是十足令人震撼哪…所以背负「火种」的半神，亦将在世界尽头成为新的泰坦，再造天地？
t_en: Heh, what a startling revelation... This means that the demigods who bear the Coreflames will also become the new Titans at the World's End, thereby shaping a new world?

t_ch: 我相信，这就是神谕中「再创世」的真相。
t_en: I believe that this is the truth behind the "Era Nova" as mentioned in the prophecy.

t_ch: 实在是…令人难以置信。
t_en: This is... utterly unbelievable.

t_ch: 而且，过去那对双子…即便她们真是{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}本尊，如今也依旧不知去向……
t_en: Besides, those twins in the past... Even if they really are {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}, their whereabouts are still unknown even today...

t_ch: 当真不知去向么？那位玻吕茜亚说过了吧，诞生了炼金与巨龙童话的海滨小城……
t_en: Are their whereabouts truly unknown? Didn't Polyxia mention that seaside town — the birthplace of alchemy and the tale of the dragon...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>斯缇科西亚</color>——在我们的世界里，也有一座城邦完美符合这描述。
t_en: In our world, there exists a city which perfectly matches that description: <color=#dbc291ff>Styxia</color>.

t_ch: 可那里信仰{RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}，和{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}并无关联。我也从未去过那里，在哀地里亚的时光…它早已是一片被淹没的废墟了。
t_en: But the people there worship {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa{RUBY_E#} and bear no relation to {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}. I have also never been to that place, as in the time of Aidonia... it had long turned into underwater ruins.

t_ch: …拿去。
t_en: ...Take this.

t_ch: 这是……
t_en: This is...

t_ch: 贤者之石，「智种学派」的杰作，以贤人的血肉为根基，将其全数智慧炼就而成的实体。
t_en: The Nousporists' masterpiece: the Philosopher's Stone. It was made by transmuting all of the Sages' wisdom into a single entity, with their flesh and blood as its foundation.

t_ch: 记得吗？我说过炼金是转化、淬炼和扬升的艺术，这也同样适用于追求真理。无论多么难以置信，世界的真相已从杂质中精炼而成。
t_en: Remember? I've mentioned before that the art of alchemy is all about conversion, tempering, and ascension. This process also applies to the pursuit of truth. Regardless of how unbelievable this sounds, the truths of the world have been refined from the impurities.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>想想巨龙童话——这枚石子定将指引你回到真正的故乡，重铸冥界大门，找到</color>{RUBY_B#<color=#dbc291ff>死亡之泰坦</color>}<color=#dbc291ff>塞纳托斯</color>{RUBY_E#}<color=#dbc291ff>的下落。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Think about the story of the dragon. This stone will lead you to your real hometown, allowing you to reform the door to the nether realm and discover</color> {RUBY_B#<color=#dbc291ff>Death Titan</color>}<color=#dbc291ff>Thanatos</color>{RUBY_E#}<color=#dbc291ff>'s fate.</color>

t_ch: 汝还是一副胜券在握的样子哪。可要是汝猜错了呢？
t_en: You still look as confident in your victory as ever, but what if your conjecture is wrong?

t_ch: 那也很简单，向全世界承认实验失败了就是。
t_en: Simple. I shall merely admit to the entire world that my experiment was a failure.

t_ch: 但我的思想绝不会失败，因为我已将「怀疑」的种子播在每个人心中。
t_en: However, my line of thinking will not fail because I have already planted the seeds of doubt into the hearts of the masses.

t_ch: 用行动证明吧，遐蝶。决议马上要开始了，别忘了你的承诺。
t_en: Actions speak louder than words, Castorice. With the assembly about to begin, don't forget the promise you've made.

t_ch: 我已走过漫长的寻索，不介意再做一次尝试。在我们交谈的当下，{NICKNAME}的生命也在一分一秒地流逝……
t_en: I've already searched for so long, I'm willing to try one more time. While we converse, {NICKNAME}'s life is also slowly slipping away...

t_ch: 但临行前，我还有一个问题：我们方才所见的一切究竟是…？
t_en: But before I leave, I have another question: What exactly did we witness just now...?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>那是烙印在{RUBY_B#卡厄斯}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}灵魂中的{RUBY_B#记忆}世界{RUBY_E#}。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>That was the {RUBY_B#memory}world{RUBY_E#} imprinted in {RUBY_B#Khaos}Kephale's{RUBY_E#} soul.</color>

t_ch: 若想证明它，也必须由你写下最后一步。去吧，别让我失望。
t_en: If we are to prove its existence, you must take the final step. Go, do not disappoint me.

t_ch: …我明白了。
t_en: ...I understand.

t_ch: 喂，人子啊……
t_en: Hey, child of humanity...

t_ch: 别心急，我一直都记得你的问题。
t_en: Don't get antsy, I still remember the question you posed to me.

t_ch: 我是个学者，不想也不能给出模棱两可的结论。等她找到证据，我们再聊这事。
t_en: As a scholar, I do not wish to offer up ambiguous conclusions. Let's discuss this topic again when she manages to find the evidence.

t_ch: 吾只是想说：那石子乃是汝用心脏炼成而来的罢？
t_en: I merely wanted to ask: Was that stone transmuted from your heart?

t_ch: 汝…已绝无法回生了哪。
t_en: If so, rebirth... is no longer an option for you.

t_ch: 有何不可？我年幼时失去了至亲，年少时放弃信仰，不久前虽被你捡回条命，却还是丢了灵魂……
t_en: Why not? When I was a child, I lost someone dear to me. When I was a teenager, I lost my faith. A while back, although you saved my life, I also lost my soul in the process...

t_ch: 现在不过是又抛弃了肉身而已，何必大惊小怪？
t_en: Now, I'm merely forsaking my body. What's the big deal?

t_ch: 为何偏要急着赴死，活着不好么？
t_en: Why are you always finding ways to die? Isn't it good to be alive?

t_ch: 不过是向至纯粹者转化时，必要的牺牲罢了。
t_en: This is but a necessary sacrifice on my path to transform into the purest of them all.

t_ch: 呵，死者能称作至纯粹者么？而且，倘若汝那猜想得以证明，岂不反坐实了神话的真实性，令汝的理论前功尽弃？
t_en: Heh, can someone who's dead even claim to be the purest of them all? Furthermore, if your hypothesis is proven correct, does it not just confirm that the myths are true and thereby invalidate all your theories?
Heh...

t_ch: 不，恰恰相反。如果我的猜想真能应验……
t_en: No, it's just the opposite. If my hypothesis is correct...

t_ch: 那也就代表——我完全可以登上神座，重塑你们创造的荒谬世界了。
t_en: It means that I can actually ascend to godhood and reshape this absurd world that you all have created.

t_ch: 操纵「浑象仪」
t_en: Operate celestial globe

t_ch: 历史发生的时序已为既定……
t_en: The sequence of historical events is already set...

t_ch: 按序揭露它的真相吧，英雄们。
t_en: Reveal its truth in order, heroes.
